focus: maintainability minimal fix proposal - Target 1 only

findingDetails.title: Minimal fix: skip only existing-parent description regeneration in append mode
findingDetails.claim: Guard the parent description generation block so it does not run when APPEND_TO_EXISTING_PARENT=true; leave normal new-parent generation and child generation unchanged.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked child generation, phase-parent detection, and phase-classifier assumptions; none require parent description regeneration during append mode.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: A generator-level hardening change could reject identity rewrites, but that would affect more callers than the observed append-mode defect.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if a runtime wrapper already prevents append-mode parent generation or if parent metadata regeneration is documented as required with parent-owned input.

findingDetails.title: Already-corrupted phase-parent metadata needs a scoped dry-run repair pass after the writer fix
findingDetails.claim: Fixing create.sh prevents future append-mode parent rewrites but does not repair the already-corrupted basename-only specFolder and empty parentChain found in iteration 3.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether the affected packet was only a child-description overwrite and whether classifiers depend on parent description regeneration; both were ruled out.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Some historical metadata drift may be unrelated, so repair must target only same-signature candidates.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if the corrupted roots are proven unreachable by all scanners/indexers or an approved repair already exists.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Non-trivial Target 1 generated-metadata writer path with existing data corruption and repair proposal scope.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Selected the confirmed writer, metadata generator, phase-parent detectors, phase classifier files named by scope, and the known corrupted packet from iteration 3.

searchLedger.hypothesis: A narrow append-mode guard around parent description generation fixes the defect without changing shared generator behavior.

searchActions.result: APPEND_TO_EXISTING_PARENT is set before FEATURE_DIR targets the parent; parent generation is separate from child generation.

searchLedger.rationale: The one-branch guard is the smallest fix matching the confirmed root cause.

searchLedger.invariant: The legitimate child description.json write must still run for each new child phase.

searchActions.result: Child block uses _child_path and is below the proposed parent-only guard.

searchLedger.rationale: The proposed guard is limited to the parent call and leaves the child loop untouched.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Verified by direct read of separate parent and child generator calls.

searchLedger.invariant: Skipping parent description generation must not break phase-parent detection or phase classification assumptions.

searchActions.result: Phase-parent detection depends on direct child folders with spec.md or description.json, not parent description.json.
The named phase classifier classifies conversation phases, not spec-folder parent/child topology.

searchLedger.rationale: The fix does not change direct child folder creation or child metadata generation, and the named classifier is unrelated to folder topology.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Existing corrupted metadata persists after the writer fix and needs a separate scoped repair pass.

searchActions.result: specFolder is basename-only and parentChain is empty.
Parent spec still describes a phase-parent packet, so the same-signature corruption is identity/lineage metadata.

searchLedger.rationale: A preventive code fix alone leaves confirmed corrupted metadata on disk.

title: Minimal fix: skip only existing-parent description regeneration in append mode
Already-corrupted phase-parent metadata needs a scoped dry-run repair pass after the writer fix

detail: Minimal fix boundary is create.sh parent generation guard keyed only to APPEND_TO_EXISTING_PARENT=true; shared generator and child write remain unchanged.
Writer path and preservation of child description generation were verified by direct reads.
deep-review skill loaded and sk-code-review review_core.md read before severity classification.

reason: Child generation uses _child_path in a separate block below the proposed parent-only guard.
isPhaseParent depends on direct child folders with spec.md or description.json, not parent description.json; phase-classifier.ts is conversation-phase classification only.
